title: Architects of Transformation
subtitle: VISIONARY LEADERSHIP DRIVING ENTERPRISE EXCELLENCE
body_text: Priti Sawant is a serial entrepreneur, category creator, and a pioneer of India’s Global Capability Center (GCC) ecosystem. With 20+ years of experience, she has built and scaled businesses at the intersection of technology and enterprise transformation. She began her journey at Accenture and later led Magna Infotech-scaling it from pre-revenue to a market leader, creating India’s first GCC-focused model, and delivering a successful exit and public listing (IPO). She is a 2-time entrepreneur, having built two successful, leading companies solving for GCCs in India.

She founded JoulestoWatts, backed by Manipal Global Education Services. Today, J2W operates across 8 countries, enabling 300+ GCCs, deploying 5,200+ professionals, and building 4 in-house products, with strong Fortune 500 partnerships. Recognised by Forbes Japan, she is also a TiE Charter Member, startup mentor, and a strong advocate of gender diversity, having built organisations with 40%+ women representation and 50% women-led leadership roles.

Priya Mohan is a seasoned operator, investor, and entrepreneur with 20+ years spanning venture capital, high-growth startups, and investment banking. She was most recently a Partner at General Catalyst India, where she led India investments and co-creation opportunities across transformative, economy-shaping companies. Previously, as Managing Partner at Venture Highway, she held board seats across marquee portfolio companies in multiple sectors. Before investing, she co-founded Vidyartha, an EdTech platform which she successfully exited to Byju's.

Her earlier career included 9 years in investment banking and equity research - structuring and executing complex transactions across private equity raises, secondary sales, and buyouts. A Chartered Accountant and Management Graduate from the Indian School of Business (ISB), Priya was a state guest of the United States under the IVLP program and is a member of the Kamalnayan Bajaj Aspen Fellowship (Cohort X).

body_text: Computer Vision  ·  Weld Inspection  ·  AI Quality  ·  100% Coverage  ·  OEM  ·  Manufacturing
Client: Top-Tier Heavy Equipment OEM   |   Domain: Manufacturing   |   Function: Quality Engineering

Heavy equipment OEM verified weld joint integrity through sampled inspection, leaving subsurface defects, porosity, and incomplete fusion undetected until they surfaced as rework, structural failures, or audit escapes. At production line speeds, manual 100% inspection was not feasible.

AI vision layer deployed at the weld station to capture and analyze every joint in real time, classifying geometry, detecting surface anomalies, and correlating findings with process parameters to isolate root cause at the joint level. Inspection moved from a sample to 100% coverage at no cost to throughput.
AI cameras capture every joint at line speed, classifying weld geometry and surface condition without sampling or line interruption.
Detects porosity, incomplete fusion, undercut, and spattering, flagging defects before parts advance to downstream assembly.
Correlates visual defects with welding parameters to isolate root cause across wire feed, voltage, and travel speed.
Links every weld to operator, shift, and material batch, generating audit-ready traceability records.
Maps defect frequency across stations and shifts, surfacing systemic quality patterns before batch escapes occur.
Routes verified defect data back to process engineering, turning every detected issue into a parameter adjustment.
Improvement in Root Cause Identification Speed

Third-party logistics operator managing thousands of daily shipments faced structural cash-flow delays in accounts receivable. Collections managers worked aging buckets, remittances arrived in mismatched formats, unapplied cash accumulated silently, and dispute resolution depended on individual analyst judgment. AI execution layer deployed over existing ERP and collections platforms. Remittances matched automatically using AI pattern recognition. Collections prioritized by predicted payment probability. Deductions and disputes auto-classified and routed. AI-drafted dunning outreach reviewed by collections team before send. All matched items staged for human confirmation before GL posting.
Auto-matches remittances to open invoices across formats and channels, including partial payments and multi-invoice remittances.
Scores every open receivable by payment likelihood, focusing collector effort where it moves cash fastest.
Classifies incoming deductions by reason code and routes to the right resolution team without manual triage.
Generates contextually appropriate follow-up communications, reviewed and released by the collections team.
Stages all matched and resolved items for human confirmation before posting to the general ledger.
Real-time visibility into DSO trends, unapplied cash balances, dispute aging, and team productivity.
